new_findings.class: residual of NF-final-closure-001 / NF-final-PII-001 / MV-cover-letter-studio-006 — the NON-LATIN-SCRIPT / NON-ASCII-LEADING-CAPITAL proper-noun case that the München (precomposed-Latin) fix does not cover
new_findings.epistemic: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] leak observed on prod @f491170 (API x3 + real browser panel); [INFERRED] root cause from code reading
new_findings.summary: JD Keyword Coverage still leaks a CamelCase concatenation artifact when the glued proper noun is written in a NON-LATIN script (Cyrillic/Greek) or BEGINS with a NON-ASCII uppercase letter (Turkish dotted-İ) — e.g. a scraped JD gluing a city to a label surfaces 'İstanbulSalary', 'КиевLocation', 'МоскваSalary', 'İzmirLocation', 'ΑθήναLocation' as TOP keyword chips on the paid intelligence rail. NF-final-closure-001's widened _WORD_RE fixed the precomposed-Latin case (München/Zürich), but the ASCII-only _CAMEL_HUMP_RE segmenter yields only the single ASCII LABEL segment for these tokens, hitting the len(segments)<2 early-return BEFORE the any-segment-is-a-label check.
reproduction: 1. Log in as a Pro user (any account with a résumé).

2. Create a Job whose scraped-style description glues a non-Latin/non-ASCII-capital city to an ASCII label with no space — e.g. 'МоскваSalary band set quarterly. İzmirLocation is hybrid. ΑθήναLocation optional. İstanbulSalary competitive.' (add some legit tech terms so the panel populates).
3. Generate (or attach) a cover letter for that job.
4. GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights → keywords.items CONTAINS the glued tokens 'МоскваSalary'/'İzmirLocation'/'ΑθήναLocation'/'İstanbulSalary' as top-ranked chips.
5. Open the letter in Cover Letter Studio → the JD Keyword Coverage panel renders the same artifact chips (screenshots/surface-a-iter1-panel-card1.png shows İstanbulSalary + КиевLocation).
new_findings.expected: No concatenation artifact surfaces as a keyword regardless of which proper noun (including non-Latin script / non-ASCII capital) is glued to the label; legit mixed-case tech preserved.
new_findings.observed: İstanbulSalary + КиевLocation leaked (novel-unicode job: run1 API, run2 fresh-session re-GET, real-browser panel). Fresh independent job reproduced МоскваSalary + İzmirLocation + ΑθήναLocation. Precomposed-Latin controls MünchenSalary/ZürichLocation AND NFD combining-diacritic München AND SãoPauloCompensation were correctly DROPPED — confirming NF-final-closure-001 works for Latin and isolating the residual to non-Latin/non-ASCII-leading-capital scripts. CJK (東京/北京) and label-first non-Latin gluings (SalaryДубай/LocationСеул) did not surface in top-10 in these runs (ranked out or filtered), so the confirmed leak class is noun-first non-Latin-script/İ proper noun + trailing ASCII label.
new_findings.root_cause_inferred: apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py: _CAMEL_HUMP_RE = re.compile(r'[A-Z](?:[A-Z]+(?=[A-Z]|$)|[a-z0-9]*)') is ASCII-only. For 'МоскваSalary' the only ASCII capital is 'S' (Salary), so _CAMEL_HUMP_RE.findall returns ['Salary'] (one segment). _is_camel_concatenation_artifact early-returns False at `len(segments) < 2` BEFORE the `any(seg.lower() in _ARTIFACT_LABEL_WORDS ...)` check ever runs, so 'МоскваSalary' passes _is_semantic_keyword and scores +45 internal-uppercase in _skill_score. The München fix widened the TOKENIZER (_WORD_RE) so accented Latin words stay whole AND still expose an ASCII capital ('M') to form a 2nd segment; it did not touch the SEGMENTER, so a proper noun with no ASCII capital contributes no hump and the label alone is the sole segment. (This is the identical early-return NF-final-closure-001 named — now reached via a different tokenizer path.)
new_findings.fix_hint: Any of: (a) in _is_camel_concatenation_artifact, drop the `len(segments) < 2` gate for the label-word branch — flag a token whose sole CamelCase segment is a label word when the token has other leading letters before it; or (b) make the uppercase class in _CAMEL_HUMP_RE Unicode-aware (regex \p{Lu} / a category test) so non-Latin/İ proper nouns produce their own segment; or (c) NFKC-normalise + transliterate non-Latin letters before segmenting so the glued token splits cleanly and hits the existing label rule.
new_findings.materiality: Narrow: requires a non-Latin-script or non-ASCII-capital place name directly glued to a label word in a scraped JD; degrades one keyword chip on the paid JD-Keyword-Coverage rail (no PII, no security, no fabricated user content — the chip is a genuine JD token, just an ugly glued one). BUT the trigger surface is arguably BROADER than NF-final-closure-001's accented-middle case — international job boards routinely emit Cyrillic/Greek/Turkish city names — and the finding's own acceptance criterion ('regardless of which proper noun is glued to the label') is violated. Filed to keep the fixers honest against that criterion; the brief explicitly asked for a novel unicode adversarial and this is the result.
evidence: uat/reports/evidence/manual-verification/adversarial/final-pass/api/repro-nonlatin-camelcase-leak.json (run2 re-GET reproduced; run3 fresh job leaked МоскваSalary/İzmirLocation/ΑθήναLocation; Latin controls dropped)

All THREE deployed fix surfaces verified as intended (surface a precomposed case clean; surface b honest refusals; surface c Scout-guidance link; surface d honest alert), and Part A gate PASSES (893/0, 477/0). The remaining defect is narrow/cosmetic (LOW, no PII/security).
